title: C919 E-TRAS — over-temp fault preempts state machine to SF (with executor-gap note)
description: Nominal deploy starts. At t=2.5s — partway through the deploy chain — etras_over_temp_fault:stuck_on injects via the A/C bus. Two protection layers fire on the next tick: (1) safety_interlock_manager preempts the state machine to SF_ABORT_OR_FAULT, (2) derive_tr_command3_enable returns False, which resets the CMD3 latch → three_phase_trcu_power_on=False. EXECUTOR GAP (deliberately documented by this fixture): compute_fadec_deploy_command in adapters/c919_etras_frozen_v1/fadec_deploy_logic.py does NOT gate on etras_over_temp_fault — it only checks engine_running, tr_inhibited, unlock_confirmed, tr_wow, n1k limit, and TRA. So fadec_deploy_command CAN re-assert as True after over_temp injects, even while state=SF. Asserting the gap explicitly here turns this fixture into a regression net: if a future change adds over_temp to compute_fadec_deploy_command (closing the gap), this fixture's t=5 assertion will FAIL and force a deliberate update — at which point the OPEN-Q-V2 (CMD3-latch §5.4) about deploy-side over-temp gating should be revisited. The system-level safety remains intact via SF preempt + CMD3 reset; the deploy-command-output value is a downstream computation, not the actuation pathway.

events.note: ETRAS controller over-temp — SF preempts immediately on next tick
SF preempts on over_temp regardless of where in the deploy cycle
CMD3 latch resets on tr_command3_enable=False (over_temp clamps it)

EXECUTOR GAP: compute_fadec_deploy_command does not gate on etras_over_temp_fault, so this stays True post-fault. Documented here so that closing the gap (adding `not over_temp` to §5.5) will FAIL this assertion and force a deliberate fixture update.
